page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom ARMv9-based AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz on the Neoverse-V2 architecture, with 192 vCPUs and no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 11400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gd.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory performance is also highly competitive, with read and write bandwidths for large blocks ranking in the top 10%. The instance excels in database and web serving workloads, achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50% range.

The instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads due to its 192 dedicated physical cores and integrated high-capacity NVMe storage. This combination eliminates the need for separate network-attached storage for high-I/O applications, though the lack of hyperthreading and moderate single-core speeds represent a tradeoff for single-threaded tasks. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by 192 dedicated physical AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of memory and 11400 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput. It also shows exceptional capability in database operations, achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations. While single-core performance is moderate, its massive parallel processing power and high-speed local storage make it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 192 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is a 192-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 384 GB RAM and 11400 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 compute instance featuring 192 dedicated cores and massive local NVMe storage for parallel workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores at 2.7 GHz.
Equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and static web serving performance.
Achieves top-tier results in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks.
Features 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and high-I/O databases.
